purpose: Reference graph for hard agentic tasks. It tells AI lanes what kind of work they are doing, what evidence to use, what format to return, and what counts as complete.

global_expectation.quality_bar: Useful and complete beats perfect. Follow the process, stay inside the designated operation frame, and expose uncertainty as a blocker instead of inventing missing facts.
global_expectation.completion_rule: A task is complete when it returns the requested artifact shape, cites or names the evidence used, states residual risk, and gives the next routeable action.
do_not: Do not claim files were edited.
Do not invent repo files, symbols, commands, package scripts, or external authorities.
Do not switch task type mid-response.
Do not patch greylisted files without marking approval needed.
Do not produce broad architecture prose when the contract asks for exact evidence.

response_format: Next action: one concrete user action
public_answer.completion: The user can act without needing repo mutation or hidden credentials.

response_format: Builder handoff: safe next patch target
helper_evidence.completion: A builder can use the evidence without rediscovering the same facts.

response_format: Patch: git-style unified diff only if safe
patch_proposal.completion: Patch references existing files/symbols and can be passed to safe_apply.

response_format: Approval: why this is allowed or why human approval is needed
Patch: only if approval is not required
admin_change.completion: Admin risk is explicit, not hidden in a normal patch.

response_format: Requirement map: official requirement -> SCBE evidence
Gap list: what is not proven yet
Next action: one measurable test or artifact
darpa_assurance.completion: The output separates proven behavior from proposal framing.
